query: Historical voter turnout percentages in Johor state elections Malaysia, especially 2013, 2018, and 2022. Also GE15 2022 Johor turnout. What is the typical turnout pattern in Johor state elections and how do analysts project turnout for the July 11 2026 Johor state election (PRN Johor)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Johor state election voter turnout:

## Key Findings

**The dominant pattern:** Johor turnout hinges on whether the state election is held *concurrently* with a federal general election or as a *standalone* poll:

| Election | Turnout | Type |
|---|---|---|
| 2013 (with GE13) | ~84–85% | Concurrent |
| 2018 (with GE14) | **82.0%** | Concurrent |
| 2022 standalone (PRN Johor) | **54.9%** | Standalone snap |
| GE15 federal, Johor (Nov 2022) | ~mid-70s% | Federal |

**The 2022 collapse (82% → 54.9%, a 27.1-point drop)** was driven by three factors: (1) the structural standalone-vs-concurrent gap, (2) COVID-19 travel restrictions blocking outstation voters (many in Singapore/KL), and (3) roll expansion from Undi18 + Automatic Voter Registration (1.8M → 2.6M voters), which mechanically lowered the percentage.

**Partisan angle:** Low turnout structurally favors incumbent BN, whose machinery reliably delivers ~600,000 votes regardless of conditions; PH's base is more federal-oriented and outstation-dependent.

**2026 (11 July, PRN Johor):** ~2.73 million eligible voters; ~1.29M under age 40. **No analyst publishes a numeric turnout projection** — they cite turnout, fence-sitters, and cost-of-living as decisive. As a standalone election it will run well below the ~82% concurrent benchmark, but — unlike 2022 — there are no COVID restrictions and both coalitions are facilitating outstation/Singapore voter return, so the trajectory points to turnout landing between the 2022 low (54.9%) and concurrent highs (~82%), likely toward the higher end of the standalone band.

The full structured findings (key facts, sources, and 16 data points) have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.
